headline: Ranked Heavyweights Clash in Desperate Bid for Momentum
subhead: Two struggling programs meet in Week 8 with bowl eligibility slipping away.

xFactor.why: Michigan's ability to generate pressure and limit explosive plays will determine whether it can contain USC's passing attack after allowing 35 points to Notre Dame.

keyMatchup: Michigan's defensive front versus USC's offensive line—the Trojans must establish rhythm to move the ball and stay ahead of the chain, while Michigan needs early stops to prevent the pace from working against them.
body: Both teams enter this contest reeling from dominant losses that exposed fundamental breakdowns. Michigan's defense surrendered 35 points to Notre Dame and struggled to generate consistent pressure, while USC's secondary was carved up in a 21-point road defeat. With bowl eligibility within reach but no margin for error, this game becomes a referendum on which staff can make immediate adjustments and impose its identity.

USC holds a slight edge in offensive continuity and has shown flashes of execution that Michigan's recent tape does not display. The Trojans' pass catchers pose a stiff test for a Michigan secondary that has been vulnerable downfield. However, Michigan's resilience in losses suggests a team that won't fold—expect a close, physical affair decided by which offense can sustain drives when the margins tighten late.
